title: SSRF defense-in-depth is well-layered
description:
The SSRF protection across the codebase is genuinely well done and worth defending against simplification:

1. is_private_host() catches known private hostnames (localhost, .local, .internal) and IP literals
2. is_private_host_resolved() does actual DNS resolution and checks all returned addresses against is_private_ip(), failing closed on DNS failure
3. reqwest client is configured with redirect::Policy::none() everywhere (server.rs:93, delivery.rs:100, feed.rs:197) preventing open-redirect SSRF
4. Checks are applied consistently at every outbound fetch point: actor cache (actor_cache.rs:47), media fetch (media.rs:56), inbox delivery (delivery.rs:150), and follower actor fetch (server.rs:722-778)
5. IPv6 handling includes mapped-v4 checks (server.rs:1174) and ULA/link-local ranges

This is three independent layers (hostname check, DNS resolution check, no-redirect policy) applied at four trust boundaries. Any one of them would be insufficient alone.
